label: Squarish 3BR / baths DISTRIBUTED with mid-band HALL-CORE (pinwheel) — master+ensuite and br3 across the rear band, br2 at front-right off the great_room; short central hall serves master, br3 and common T&B. Open-plan great_room + kitchen (gr style).

adjacencies.note: ensuite is an alcove in master's rear-left corner — interior door from master
ensuite sits directly behind the kitchen head wall — first wet stack
master door opens south into the hall — 1.0 m floor = 0.8 m clear + frame stops
br3 door opens south into the hall
common T&B door opens west into the hall — second wet stack, kept one bay from the kitchen run
bath block is the acoustic buffer between hall/wet zone and br2
br2 door opens directly off the great_room (front-right) — saves ~1 m of hall; the privacy trade-off of this family
hall's south end is an open mouth into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)
great_room + kitchen are one open LDK (gr style)

soft_proximities.note: pull the common bath toward the kitchen run to shorten the second plumbing branch
br2 has no hall access — keep its bath close

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_sq_hall_core_baths_ds_hall_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: 3BR programs bite earlier than 2BR, so the profile triggers at 110 m² instead of 100. auto_apply floors the galley kitchen (1.8 m > PD 1096's 1.5 m hard min), the hall clear width (0.95 m > 0.90 m corridor hard min) and both baths, and caps master at 16 m² so the standard pair never drops below ~9 m². preferred_apply bumps ensuite to 3.0 m² and the kitchen to a 2.0 m galley where feasible.

notes: 3BR squarish catalog topology #1 — HALL-CORE (pinwheel). Natural floor band
~90-102 m²; minimum feasible shell 9.0 x 9.5 m buildable (13 x 13.5 lot,
ncp/rectangular envelope) or 9.5 x 10.5 m buildable (13.5 x 14.5 lot, ccp; re-tuned 2026-07-19 when the master-bigger rule was extended to ALL standard bedrooms —
the front-right carport notch costs ~0.5 m of both dimensions). Verified
2026-07-18 end-to-end (solve + validate COMPLIANT) for ncp, ccp right,
ccp left (mirror) and fcp.
Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_side='right'):
Rear:  [ensuite : master        | br3         ]
Mid:   [kitchen  | HALL         | common T&B  ]
Front: [great_room (entry)      | br2         ]
Program: public band at front-left (great_room hosts entry, kitchen behind it),
private band across the rear (master with ensuite alcove at the rear-left
corner, br3 rear-right), br2 tucked at front-right. A short mid-band hall
(~1.2 x 2.5 m) between kitchen and common bath serves master, br3 and the
common T&B; its south mouth is open to the great_room.
Ventilation: all three bedrooms and the great_room hold exterior walls
(master/br3 rear, br2 front) — NBC Rule 7/8 light-and-vent satisfied with no
landlocked habitable room, the usual failure mode of squarish 3BR layouts.
Plumbing: two short runs — ensuite backs onto the kitchen head wall (stack 1),
common T&B sits one bay east off the hall (stack 2), pulled toward the kitchen
by a soft proximity. Dirty kitchen + laundry land in the rear setback behind
the kitchen.
Trade-off vs the side_split sibling: br2's door opens off the great_room
(bedroom_to_public) instead of the hall — shorter circulation, slightly less
bedroom privacy. When brief.carport_side='left', mirror the whole topology.
Solver notes (2026-07-18): kitchen_side_pin=false is REQUIRED — the kitchen
column is left_anchored (non-carport side), which contradicts the default
canonical-orientation pin outright and made the topology infeasible at every
lot size. `common` is deliberately NOT in right_anchored: pinning it to the
right wall while it must also reach hall (west door) and buffer br2 was a
combinatorial contradiction; the bath-at-exterior-wall soft bonus still
pulls common toward the right wall when geometry allows.
